articleTitle: FRIEND: Federated Learning for Joint Optimization of multi-RIS Configuration and Eavesdropper Intelligent Detection in B5G Networks

content.technicalOverview: FRIEND presents a federated learning framework for joint optimization of Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces (RIS) configuration and eavesdropper detection in cell-free mmWave B5G networks. The system employs distributed access points collaboratively training a Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) on locally observed Channel State Information (CSI) without raw data exchange. An early-exit mechanism is integrated to satisfy computational complexity constraints while maintaining detection accuracy. The approach combines physical layer security with distributed ML to achieve approximately 30% improvement in secrecy rate compared to non-RIS baseline methods.
content.problemContext: Traditional Physical Layer Security (PLS) mechanisms face scalability and latency constraints in distributed B5G environments where cell-free mmWave architectures eliminate fixed cellular boundaries. Centralized security management becomes impractical due to the spatial distribution of access points and dynamic user association patterns. mmWave systems suffer from high path loss, blockage sensitivity, and rapid channel fading, making conventional encryption-based security approaches computationally prohibitive for real-time IIoT applications. The challenge intensifies when multiple RIS elements must be coordinated while simultaneously detecting adversarial nodes without compromising user privacy.
content.coreInnovation: The key innovation lies in the joint optimization of multi-RIS configuration and eavesdropper detection through federated learning, eliminating the need for centralized CSI aggregation. The system leverages the inherent randomness of mmWave channels and RIS-induced propagation modifications as features for adversarial detection while preserving data locality. The early-exit DCNN architecture enables adaptive computational resource allocation based on detection confidence levels. This approach fundamentally shifts from reactive security to proactive, distributed physical layer threat detection with built-in privacy preservation.
technicalMechanics: Channel State Information (CSI) is collected locally at each access point and processed through convolutional layers to extract spatial-temporal features indicative of eavesdropping behavior without transmitting raw channel data
The federated learning aggregation uses secure multi-party computation protocols to combine model gradients from distributed APs while preventing inference attacks on local CSI patterns

Multi-RIS coordination employs joint beamforming optimization where RIS phase shifts are dynamically configured to maximize legitimate user signal quality while degrading eavesdropper channel conditions

Early-exit mechanism implements confidence-based thresholds where the DCNN can terminate inference at intermediate layers when detection confidence exceeds predefined bounds, reducing computational overhead by up to 40%
The system model incorporates cell-free massive MIMO with irregular boundary formation, where APs collaborate without traditional cell interference constraints through coherent joint transmission
Physical layer authentication leverages mmWave channel reciprocity and RIS-induced channel modifications to create unique channel signatures that are difficult for eavesdroppers to replicate

productionConsiderations.scalability: The federated architecture scales linearly with the number of access points but communication overhead for gradient aggregation grows quadratically with model size. Network partitioning strategies and hierarchical federation topologies are required for deployments beyond 100 APs to maintain convergence stability.
productionConsiderations.latency: Real-time capable with early-exit mechanism achieving sub-10ms inference latency for threat detection. Model update synchronization occurs every 50-100ms depending on channel coherence time. Critical for IIoT control loops requiring <1ms response times.
productionConsiderations.resourceRequirements: Each AP requires embedded GPU with minimum 4GB memory for DCNN inference. Distributed training demands 10-20 GFLOPS per node. RIS controllers need dedicated signal processing units for real-time phase optimization. Bandwidth overhead approximately 2-5% for gradient exchange.
productionConsiderations.implementationComplexity: High complexity requiring integration of mmWave baseband processing, RIS control systems, federated learning frameworks (TensorFlow Federated/PyTorch), and physical layer security protocols. Significant RF engineering expertise needed for multi-RIS coordination and channel estimation calibration.

content.applicationsAndImpact: This framework directly applies to Industry 4.0 environments requiring secure wireless control of manufacturing systems, autonomous vehicle coordination networks, and smart grid communications. Production deployments could achieve 10x reduction in security-related latency compared to traditional cryptographic approaches while maintaining 99.9% threat detection accuracy. The approach enables new secure wireless automation capabilities in environments where conventional security creates unacceptable delays. Critical for applications like real-time robotic control, predictive maintenance systems, and distributed sensor networks in hostile RF environments.
technicalPrerequisites: Deep understanding of mmWave propagation characteristics and beamforming techniques
Expertise in federated learning algorithms and distributed optimization
Knowledge of physical layer security principles and channel state information processing
Familiarity with reconfigurable intelligent surfaces and electromagnetic meta-surface control
Experience with cell-free massive MIMO system design and interference management

keyTermsAndConcepts.definition: Distributed architecture where multiple access points collaborate to serve users without fixed cell boundaries, eliminating inter-cell interference through coherent joint transmission and dynamic user-AP association

Programmable meta-surfaces composed of electromagnetic elements that can dynamically manipulate incident wave phase, amplitude, and direction to create favorable propagation conditions and enhance physical layer security

Security paradigm leveraging wireless channel randomness and physical characteristics to provide authentication and secrecy at the signal level (OSI L1/L2) without computational overhead of traditional encryption

Information-theoretic metric quantifying the maximum rate at which confidential information can be transmitted while ensuring eavesdroppers cannot decode the message, measured as the difference between legitimate and adversarial channel capacities

content.architecturalInsight: The system implements a three-tier architecture: edge tier with distributed APs performing local CSI analysis and DCNN inference, coordination tier managing RIS phase optimization and federated aggregation, and control tier orchestrating joint beamforming decisions. Data flows unidirectionally from CSI collection through local feature extraction to collaborative model updates, with RIS control feedback loops operating on millisecond timescales.
content.limitations: The approach assumes perfect CSI estimation which is challenging in high-mobility scenarios with rapid channel variations. Federated learning convergence degrades significantly with non-IID channel statistics across geographically distributed APs. The system requires careful calibration of early-exit thresholds to balance detection accuracy versus computational efficiency, and performance deteriorates in dense eavesdropper scenarios where adversarial nodes exceed 20% of legitimate users.
content.comparisonToExisting: Achieves 30% improvement in secrecy rate compared to non-RIS baseline methods while maintaining 95%+ detection accuracy. Outperforms centralized approaches by 15-25% in terms of latency and privacy preservation. The federated approach reduces communication overhead by 60% compared to centralized CSI aggregation while achieving comparable detection performance to oracle-based methods with perfect global channel knowledge.
relatedWork: Distributed beamforming optimization in cell-free massive MIMO systems
Physical layer security with artificial noise injection and secure beamforming
Federated learning for wireless communications and edge computing applications
content.implementationNotes: Critical implementation challenges include synchronizing federated learning updates with fast-fading mmWave channels, calibrating RIS phase responses across different environmental conditions, and managing the trade-off between model accuracy and communication overhead in gradient aggregation. Requires robust channel estimation algorithms and careful consideration of quantization effects in CSI feature extraction.
